---
title: "llm_classifier_results"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-02"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)


prereg_classified <- read_csv("prereg_classified.csv")

# add JEPLMC data
prereg_classified_JEPLMC <- read_csv("prereg_classified_JEPLMC.csv") %>% 
  select(-`Journal Title`)

# combine these into one df
prereg_classified <- bind_rows(prereg_classified, prereg_classified_JEPLMC)

data_key <- bind_rows(
  read_excel("PsycArticles/data_key (1).xlsx", sheet = "2020"),
  read_excel("PsycArticles/data_key (1).xlsx", sheet = "2021"),
  read_excel("PsycArticles/data_key (1).xlsx", sheet = "2022"),
  read_excel("PsycArticles/data_key (1).xlsx", sheet = "2023"),
  read_excel("PsycArticles/data_key (1).xlsx", sheet = "2024"),
  read_excel("PsycArticles/data_key (1).xlsx", sheet = "2025")
)

```

```{r}
prereg_classified <- left_join(prereg_classified,
                                 data_key %>% 
                                  select(DOI, `Journal Title`),
                                  by = "DOI") %>%  # add journal title to our dataset
                            rename(journal_title = `Journal Title`)

# Count the number of articles that use the OSF preregistration template for each journal (we need at least 20 per journal)
prereg_classified %>% 
  group_by(journal_title) %>% 
  filter(uses_osf_template == TRUE) %>% 
  summarise(n_articles = n_distinct(DOI)) %>% 
  ungroup()

# Save those that use the prereg template in a separate dataframe
prereg_template <- prereg_classified %>% 
  filter(uses_osf_template == TRUE)

```


```{r}
# Randomly sample 20 articles per journal
set.seed(28042026) # For reproducibility

sample <- prereg_template %>% 
  group_by(DOI) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 1) %>%  # first select one study per article 
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(journal_title) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 20) %>%  # then sample 20 articles per journal
  select(c(DOI, reasoning, study_number, all_registration_urls, journal_title)) %>% 
  ungroup()
  

# Assign two coders to each article, ensure the same coder doesn't appear as both coder 1 and coder 2
n <- nrow(sample)
coders <- c("BC", "JC", "EK", "SaB", "SiB")

# Generate all possible pairs where coder_1 != coder_2
pairs <- expand.grid(coder_1 = coders, coder_2 = coders) %>%
  filter(coder_1 != coder_2)

# Repeat pairs to cover all rows and shuffle
pairs <- pairs[rep(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), length.out = nrow(sample)), ]
pairs <- pairs[sample(nrow(pairs)), ]

# Assign to sample
sample$coder_1 <- pairs$coder_1
sample$coder_2 <- pairs$coder_2


# Check balance
table(sample$coder_1)
table(sample$coder_2)

# Check if any coder was assigned to coder_1 and coder_2 for a single article
any(sample$coder_1 == sample$coder_2)
```

```{r}
# Add article number for our reference
sample <- sample %>% 
  mutate(article_number = row_number()) %>% 
  select(-reasoning)
```
